operationalSituations.description: Vehicle driving with an active LIN sub-bus controlling body electronics (windows, mirrors, seats, wipers, climate flaps).
Vehicle stationary, ignition on, comfort and convenience LIN functions active.
Diagnostic session in progress using LIN master request (0x3C) and slave response (0x3D) frames during service or production.
Industrial LIN segment driving actuators (valves, motors, lighting) under continuous master schedule execution.

hazards.description: Master transmits a header for the wrong frame ID — an unintended slave actuates the wrong actuator.
A corrupted LIN frame payload is received without error detection and an incorrect command is delivered to an actuator.
Incorrect PID parity allows a wrong frame ID to be accepted as valid.
A checksum error is not detected and corrupted data is passed to the application.
An LDF signal is decoded with wrong bit offsets and an actuator is set to an out-of-range value.
An E2E sequence-counter gap is not detected — a replayed or lost safety frame goes unnoticed.

safetyGoals.description: go-LIN shall correctly identify frame IDs using PID parity computation and verification.
safetyGoals.safeState: Frame with an unverifiable or mismatched PID is rejected and not delivered to the application.

safetyGoals.description: go-LIN shall detect frame payload corruption using the LIN checksum algorithm.
safetyGoals.safeState: Frame failing checksum verification is rejected and reported as an error rather than delivered.

safetyGoals.description: go-LIN shall correctly parse LDF signal definitions and decode frame payloads without offset errors.
safetyGoals.safeState: Malformed LDF input is rejected at parse time with a descriptive error; no decode occurs.

safetyGoals.description: go-LIN shall detect E2E sequence gaps and CRC mismatches.
safetyGoals.safeState: E2E-protected frame with a sequence gap or CRC mismatch is surfaced as an E2EError and not treated as valid.

safetyGoals.description: go-LIN shall validate all frame IDs and data lengths at API boundaries.
safetyGoals.safeState: Out-of-range frame ID or data length is rejected by ValidateFrame before transmission or processing.
